steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage establishes two premises: (1) behavioral differences between breeds are explained by genetic differences between those breeds — especially for attachment and attention-seeking; (2) French bulldogs and cairn terriers differ behaviorally in attachment and attention-seeking. The correct inference must follow from both premises together.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the French bulldog and the cairn terrier differ with respect to the genetic underpinnings for attachment and attention-seeking.': Directly follows: behavioral difference in that trait → genetic difference underlies it, per Study 1. ✓
  • Choice B — 'the French bulldog and the cairn terrier will likely become more genetically similar over time.': Neither study addresses future genetic change. ✗
  • Choice C — 'French bulldogs and cairn terriers show a greater tendency toward attachment and attention-seeking than most other dog breeds do.': The studies compare French bulldogs to cairn terriers, not to all other breeds. ✗
  • Choice D — 'individual French bulldogs may display higher levels of attachment and attention-seeking than individual cairn terriers.': The studies address breed-level genetics and breed-level behavior, not individual variation within breeds. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
